I am legitimate. I am worthy. I am beautiful. 

thank you for granting me the gift of awareness.

you are a genius. 

you know me better than I know myself. 

& now, you're teaching me to be even more real. to let down my guard & share bloopers. to be stupid & silly & weird & “imperfect”. 

I don't have to be--or seem to be-- XYZ to “be anything”. 

I can just be [myself].

I am grateful for the opportunity to become legible to myself. I am grateful for the opportunity to love myself more. I am grateful for all that you've taught me. I am grateful for how you ingeniously created ways of protecting me from unwanted or excessive attention.

as a child, I knew the world’s judgements so intimately that I internalised them as my own. I wore others’ criticism like a sword to prevent anyone else from getting close. I didn't want to be attacked; I wanted to be safe. that's beautiful too. IT ALL MAKES SENSE.

we hide from the things we don't want to see when we aren't ready to face them. I wasn't ready before; I am now. things have changed. change can be scary & that's okay; that's part of the adventure. you show me--often viscerally--that I am allowed to be vulnerable too. thank you.

I thought I knew my place. it hurt. 

… but you weren't the cause of that. I see now that you simply wanted to protect me from additional pain. 

when no one else was there, you were my friend in the dark.

I don’t know if you remember, but we have a long history. for most of my early life, I felt ashamed of my chubby cheeks & “weird” body. I thought that no one could or would ever truly love or be attracted to me. I defined myself in relation to how I thought men saw & valued me.

I am sad that I rejected you. I didn't understand that you had a rightful place. I didn't want to see that you were important, beautiful & sacred. it was too scary for me. just as seeing myself [for all I was] was too scary for me too. we are but mirrors.

I know you want me to be happy. I know you want me to be able to live freely. thank you for being by my side throughout my life. thank you for teaching me to be kind to myself in moments of vulnerability. thank you for creating opportunities for others to be kind to me.

I love you, and I don't think you're wrong. 

I know it can be hard to see & be seen. 

I know how painful it is to be rejected, or be confronted with an image of yourself that doesn't tally w your mental perception of who you are or want to be.

I appreciate you being here w me.

in some deep, essential, profound way... 

YOU LOVE ME [too].

you want to make sure that I'm okay & you want to make sure that nothing happens that I'm not okay with. 

you keep me guarded. you don't want me to see or do anything that I’m not ready for the consequences of. 

YOU DON’T WANT TO KEEP ME SMALL.

you want me to be & feel secure.

I love you so deeply. 

that's the first thing. 

I love you so deeply & I treasure you. 

you want to keep me safe.

I love you. thank you for everything.
the night breeze is soft. I move, I waft. some dreams never die, and some dreams are only just beginning. “such is life”-- and one more time, with feeling.
writing these letters is calming for me. there is a stillness to it, a universality. I can see what I couldn't see before. I can feel what I couldn't feel before. nothing is wrong, and nothing hurts.
I want you to know that I'm also here to stay.

I hope you feel proud of me one day. I trust that you will, in your own way. I want you to know that I see what you sacrificed. I want you to know that I see the love in your pain. and, most of all, I want you to know that it won't go to waste.
the things we go through in life make us & shape us. they do not have to break us. we are not broken. we are already saved.
I feel sad that I spent so much time wanting you to be different. I feel sad that I couldn't see you before. I feel sad about how selfish I was. and I don't hate myself for it at all.

your love for me is the purest of all. you struggle, and you stay. you've questioned it, and you don't back away. miraculously, we have never been too far gone.  like everything else: it could’ve been so different.

I don't speak enough about how inspired I am by you. I don't speak enough about how much you've taught & shaped me. I don't speak enough about how indebted I am to you. I wouldn't be a cycle-breaker without you. I owe you everything.

you taught me the meaning of love because you stayed. you stay. you stay even when (or though) it's hard. our relationship is my greatest role model. we don't have to like, be the same as or understand one another to be able to love.
love is present in the strangest, most obvious & most unexpected of places-- often at the same time.

8 words to blast a man’s heart open:

I loved you. I love you. I'm sorry.

I'm realising that I love in ways that don't make sense. I can either guard my heart against that-- against my own or other people’s judgements & projections-- or I can trust my feelings & let myself be. I can trust my feelings & how it feels. I am happiest living open-heartedly.

it felt like you saw me, too. it felt like you understood & were a part of my world in a way I’d never experienced before. I didn't want anything from you. I wasn't angry with you. I was happy for you. I wanted you to be happy too.
we listened to k’s voice together & gently laughed in the dark. you said you liked the meditations. I said how much it touched me that you liked them. it felt like you liked me, too.
there is something about seeing you again that feels bittersweet. I write to you like you can hear me. you can, you do... and everyone else can too. I’m not self-conscious anymore. it's okay.
there is nowhere to hide anymore. that's the first thing. the second thing is that I don't want to. I don't want to hide anymore. I want to soften.

every time I have been (or felt) betrayed, it's bc I gave my power away. I gave my power away bc I wanted to & I thought it was the right thing to do. I didn't give my power away; I gave my power *to* you. I gave you my power bc I trusted you & I trusted you would keep it safe.

hatred is dissociative. when we stay with hate in a safe container for long enough, the whole body starts to join in. feelings start to pour in, and the whole body wakes up. hatred thawed is heartbreak.

I just hopped off a call with my soul sisters. I have so much love in my life. I have so much love in my heart. & I have myself: my greatest love of all. I have myself in the deepest way possible. I am whole. I know what it feels like to belong.

I want to normalise & celebrate feminine expression & power in all its forms. I am soft. I am so fucking soft it’s fucking ridiculous. no, I don't let anyone walk over me. yes, I am good with money. yes, I am dangerous. yes, I love myself. no, I will never try to hurt anyone.

it is painful to be unseen, unheard & misunderstood. but it isn't painful in an egoic sense. it's painful in a soul sense. for so long, I’ve wanted to find my tribe, my people, my community. I've desired more. I know what I am capable of. and... it isn't about me.

your poetry moves me to tears. there is a sense of the whimsical, the metaphysical, a stream of consciousness that is not my own. I relinquish any need or desire to know. I laugh. I roll in the dust. I am home.

sometimes I wonder if I will be forever young. I feel like I'm ageing backwards; time confuses me. it’s all silly. it’s all perfect. I feel like I was born old: I saw too much; I knew too much; I was never allowed to be a child. I watch the veil be stripped away.

I'm touched by the magic & humbled by it, too. it means so much to me to be seen for my innocence. it means so much to me to be seen in my innocence. I want to love, I want to be loved & I am love. it hurts when I am not--or can't be seen in-- that way.
there is a deeper wisdom & knowing. there is a song, a dance, a stream flowing. we are remembering. we are becoming. we are coming home to ourselves, to who we really are. can you feel it?

side note: for the sake of my inner child, I’m not going to delete this 🧵 & repost it wo typos/grammatical errors.I don't have to be perfect here either. it isn't just that no one expects that of me; it's that NO ONE WANTS IT OF ME. perfection is the only thing that’s unwanted.

this is how I feel into & alchemise shame, in real-time. I notice what’s alive & I follow it.  I feel deep grief. I feel fear. I feel rage. I feel sad. I feel worry. I’m grateful to be able to connect with, hold space for & love myself. I'm grateful to be able to feel.

I am loved, loveable & worthy of love just as I am. 

I am wanted. 
I want all of me. 

you are wanted. 
I want all of you.

I don't have to be perfect to be loved, loveable or worthy of love. 

you don't either.

the littlest things mean the most. 

these small moments of breaking open. 

tenderness. humility. connection. hope. fear. 

these are the moments we all live for, at some level. these are the moments that remind us that we are alive.

I am love. 
I am loved. 
I am guided.
I am free. 

I am love. 
I am loved. 
I am guided as me.

I don't have to know or do everything. 

I don't have to know or do anything. 

I am allowed to be where I am. 
I am okay, right where I am.

I am really good at being me. 

and... 

I am really good *as* me. 
I am good as I am. 

I don't need to change or hide anything. 

I am safe. I am good. I can rest. 
I am peaceful.

I am reminded of how other people see me. 

my joyful messiness, foibles & idiosyncrasies are what make me beautiful-- not the other way around. 

my imperfection is beautiful. 
my clumsiness is beautiful. 
my childish ignorance & occasional impulsivity are adorable.

I learned to be hard on myself as a survival mechanism. 

the gifts of softness, love & compassion have been a revelation to me. 

for (what felt like) a long time, I had to be perfect. there wasn't space for anything else.

she blames herself for all of this. 

she has to; she has no other reference point. she has no one to speak to about this. 

she has been told that she is a monster & she has no one to comfort her.

this child also doesn't feel safe to vulnerable. this child senses that if she were to express herself more fully, she would be unwanted, cast out & rejected. unconsciously, she resolves to do whatever she can to stop that from happening. she has to. it's her only choice.
deep inside me, there is a child who believes she has to be perfect to be loved. she doesn't believe this because she wants to. she believes this because she has evidence of it.

my inner child is crying out for my forgiveness because my inner child blames herself for everything that happened to her. 

my inner child didn't understand that the adults around her didn't know what to do with (or how to take care of) her. 

she thought it was her fault.

when I feel shame, my inner child needs me. 

she needs love, warmth, kindness & tenderness. she needs to know that she is safe & worthy.

she needs to know I’m not angry with or disappointed in her. 

she needs to know that I forgive her. 

she is crying out for my forgiveness.

I crave the loving arms of my inner parents, to make up for the parents who weren’t here. 

I crave something I never had. 

I crave something I learned to give at such deep (gut-wrenching) cost. 

I crave my own love. 

I want it to pour.

I’m not angry & upset with myself because “I got it wrong”. 

I’m angry & upset with myself because “I knew better”. 

I feel most insecure when I do MYSELF a disservice. 

in that moment, I crave comfort, validation & safety in the way that I didn’t receive when I was little.

they remind me of all the moments where I suddenly had the rug pulled out from under my feet & lost people who were close to me. 

they remind me of being a kid in primary school-- the pretentious, try-hard Hermione Grainger outcast who felt (feared) she was hard to love.

I know that tomorrow I will laugh at this. I know that this small faux pas is so tiny that the other person probably didn’t even notice it, let alone care. but I care. & I care. moments like this scare me.

the vast majority of the time, I am so good at noticing & moving through my emotions that this doesn’t come up. 

which makes small moments like this-- the things that slip through the net and make me feel stupid & pathetic (a HUGE rarity for me) all the more gut-wrenching.

I still carry a wound that I am too much. 

sometimes, I'll respond to something very quickly from a dysregulated place & then my adult self will catch up. I can't take it back. 

it says, “what have you done?!?!?” 

sometimes, I can do something about it. 

other times, I can't.

despite the below, I still feel shame sometimes. 

of course, I do. 

... and what’s ironic, apt & (dare I say) classic is that I experienced it within 5 minutes of writing this. 

of course, I did. 

a bittersweet reminder of my humanity. https://t.co/prpqaCGFjB

the enemy is not honesty.

the truth: 

historically, sharing my “negative emotions” often made things worse for me. 

some people stepped in to try & fix it. 

some people stepped in to try & fix me. 

others met me with pity. 

I usually felt more alone & misunderstood. 

I still carry that grief.

I rarely feel lonely. 

I notice that when I do, I tend to withdraw from the world. 

I go inward & cushion myself for rejection. I don't want to be seen.

right now, I'm making a point to do something differently:

right now, I feel lonely.

I'm happy, healthy, loved & FEELING.

it's important to me that this account is a living, breathing expression of my heart, soul & fire. 

this is a piece of art. 

this is a testimony to the power of art & the power of human connection.

this is a devotional prayer to the power of love. 

I love it here.

I don't want to lose sight of that. 

I don't want to become disconnected from that. 

I don't want to stop sharing & expressing the innate vulnerability of my (our) humanity in truthful & multi-faceted ways.

I am continually coming up against my own residual conditioning about how I am allowed to express myself here, particularly as it relates to my safety.

I care about taking care of myself. 

I will always be my #1 priority. 
I protect myself fearlessly. 

I am vulnerable too.

self-deception is a form of self-censorship. 

the things we don't allow ourselves to acknowledge are also the things that we don't allow ourselves to express.

I would be lying if I said that I wasn't a deeply-feeling woman who yearned to be met in new ways.

I'm not going to lie anymore.

big idea #2: 

you have to know what you don't want to be able to identify what you DO want 

crucially, you have to know it in an INTEGRATED way 

how to learn this: 

- pay attention to your body
- find a way of putting it into words 

(aka, making it legible for yourself)

key point: 

“no, I don't want to” creates space for an “and, I want to”

why? 

it's now clear to ALL PARTIES that you don't want to do X. 

so, the logical next question is: 

“well, what DO you want?” 

... at which point, your imaginations/s can kick into gear!

saying “no, I don't want to” is a gateway to more authentic & intentional connection

now

- you know what you don't want 
- they know what you don't want 
- you know they know what you don't want

you're on the same page!

big idea #1: 

saying “no” isn’t rude. it’s KIND. 

it's kind because it's clear. 

saying “no, I don't want to” (if that's the reason why you're saying no) is even better. 

why? 

it provides the OP with valuable insight into what you do & don't want to be available for.

I've known that “no” is a full sentence for a long time, but I've rarely approached the act of saying no so bluntly--particularly with people I’m close to. 

in practice,I’ve cared more about managing and/or placating their feelings [about me] than being in integrity with myself.

I'm learning, experientially, that I'm allowed to say no to anything that doesn't feel right for me & it feels liberating. 

it's different when it's EMBODIED. 

aka: 
- when I feel grounded & legitimate in my right to say it 
- when I’m not losing energy trying to justify myself

what happened?

today, I want to write a letter to my favourite person in the entire world: 

this little girl. 

everything I do now is devoted to her. 

everything I am now is bc of her. 

she taught me to be brave, strong & powerful bc she taught me how to take care of her.

SHE NEEDED ME. https://t.co/T2nfy6XJSm

when I think about the ways I used to lose myself in others, I return to [being] that little girl. 

that little girl who was gaslit into believing she had no sense of who she was. 

she did, she did, she did. 

she always knew.

I always knew.

[my] life has been my greatest gift, teacher & blessing. 

I am my own gift, teacher & blessing. 

not because I am alone. 

not because I do or have done this alone. 

because I know how to hold myself. 

I am my own soulmate
& I know how to love me.

I used to be a Seeker. 

I think we all are, at heart, in one way or another. 

it's what happens when we are taught to look outside ourselves for answers. 

it's what happens when we are taught to distrust [our own] nature. 

there are no accidents.

BARE*

I mean BARE! 🥹

... yes, I'm fucking unbearable 🤣

... and I'm not deleting/rewriting this thread, on principle 

all of me is here to stay, including adorable typos 😇

in the process of getting to know me, you will see yourself w new eyes. you will experience yourself differently. you will come alive. 

this is not hyperbole. 

the screenshot = from a friend (shared w permission). 

I've touched his heart deeply too.

it's what I'm here to do. https://t.co/o08jGYrG5r

to be clear: I don't just (or even necessarily) mean physically.

I mean spiritually. 

you will get to know me on an intimate level.

do you *want* to do that? 

(no is good! 😘)

these things are not for the faint of heart bc they require you to allow yourself to be seen by me. 

and... 

real relationships require reciprocity.

the deeper we go, 
the more you will see of me too. 

are you ready for that?

working with me is not for the faint of heart. 

spending time with me is not for the faint of heart. 

loving me is not for the faint of heart. 

but not because I’m “bad” or scary. 

by pretty universal accounts [*looks around nervously*, lol], I’m a joy to be around.

they could choose to walk away & never see me again & I would still love them. 

I understand what it takes to bear your soul to another.

no matter what: I have so much respect & admiration for them.

bc I am not scared of them or their love-- what it means or represents, or how it could constrain me-- I am able to access new levels of compassion. 

I'm able to connect & stay connected. I’m able to ground & stay grounded. 

and... 

I'm able to love *truly* unconditionally.

& what happens in that moment is almost miraculous. 

I’m not running. I’m not hiding. 
I’m not placating or denying. 

I’m not uncomfortable with their love. 

I can RECEIVE it. 
I can BASK in it. 

& that feels good for us both.

I can hold & FEEL us both. 

I am not scared. 

I don't need to run away from anything or anyone. 

not the situation, not the other person & not myself. 

I am guided by something beyond words. 

I know what to do. 

I have a lot of practice with it.

and... there's a new twist. 

I am safe. I am in control. I am RESPONSIBLE. 

the cleanliness of the dynamic allows me to step into my power in new ways.

and, in the process... 

I notice my own fears & limitations dissolve. 

I notice my own conceptions about what is & isn't OK (or “safe”) come into play.

there are many kinds of love. 

I view my role as a facilitator & co-creator of transformational experiences & relationships with reverence. 

I treat my clients’ feelings with tenderness. I hold space for them as I do myself.

I've always had a very expansive definition of love & intimacy. 

I’ve always wanted to live that way, too. I've wanted to be able to. it’s my purest expression. it sets me free, too.

a question I get asked a lot is: 

“what do you do if clients fall in love with you?” 

for me, love isn’t a problem. it doesn't have to get in the way. 

I feel love too. 
I feel love back. 

it gets to be easy.

& still…

I have never regretted allowing myself to be [seen to be] ugly, vulnerable & human.

you are beloved too.

you don't have to choose me.

you don't have to trust me. 
I trust me. 

you don't have to love me.

I don't need to explain anymore. 
I don't have to justify anything else.

everything is good.
everything is magical. 

I am well. I am clear. I am cleansed. 
I am safe. I am love[d]. I am free[d].

I'm enjoying my own quietness. 

relishing it, even. 

I get me first. 
I choose me first. 
I choose me always. 

I don't want to be anything, anywhere or anyone else.

I don't want to talk about what's happened or changed.

I want to keep some things for myself. that clarity is sacred. 

and, of course... 

I'm still here. 
I’m still staying. 

I, you, we can hold multitudes. we’re in this together.

when we are taught to be afraid of who we are, we’re also taught to resist trying to find out why. 

the “why” behind this is simple: 

we’re afraid of what we might find.
when we are taught to be ashamed of who we are, we are also taught not to question why.

I could say that I used to be so ashamed of & disgusted by my body that I [couldn't look at it naked &] went on a one-woman mission to destroy myself. 

that would be true. 

and... this isn't about the body (or my body).

this is about shame.

I love my body. 

I love my life. 
I love my heart. 

I love the purity with which I am able to give. 

I love that I am my best asset & competitive advantage. 

I love how deeply I know, trust & appreciate myself. 

I treasure who I am & who I was. 

I’m so grateful for that.

I wasn’t unhappy before.

I just didn't know that this was actually possible bc I hadn't experienced it.

now, I have. 
now, I know it is. 

learning is experiential. 

we need to [be able to] FEEL the difference to [be able to] LEARN & DISCERN the difference. 

it’s that simple.

what's on my mind right now is the extent to which things have changed. 

I am not who I was this time last week. 

every day is more easeful & cohesive than the one before. 

I love it.

I want to talk about how it feels to be more private here. 

I owe you nothing. 
you owe me nothing. 

it's how we can BOTH choose to stay.

I want to talk about how triumphant I feel. 

I want to talk about how much this journey & initiation has required of & from me. 

I want to talk about the fact that I was right all along. 

I want to own my worth, my power, my knowledge & my wisdom shamelessly.

and I do.

I do.

the mirage cracks. 
the dam breaks. 
the voice speaks. 

“I can see clearly now the rain has gone.”

this moment will not last forever, but I am here to stay. 

I love you. hold on.

I don't know if I'm a prophet or if I was just right all along. 

but either way-- the things I say have a habit of being (or becoming) true.

I'm a mirror,but my gift is that I am not just a pane of reflective glass. 

I bring out the best in people. 
I give them space to shine.

I am not who I was this time last year. 

and...I love her even more because I am not her anymore. 

I can hold things she couldn't. 
I can do things she couldn't. 
I can be things she couldn't. 
I can feel things she couldn't. 

I can be everything.
and... I can let myself be.

I will treasure how deeply I loved. 

I will treasure how strong I became.

I will treasure how willing I was to love (& lose) along the way. 

I will treasure everything because I treasure everything now.

I will treasure how willing I was to die. 

I will treasure how willing I was to kill. 

I will treasure how willing I was to grieve. 

I will treasure how willing I was to feel.
I will treasure how I refused to let myself settle for less than I wanted or deserved, from myself or anyone else.
I will treasure how I held my own in a world that wasn't going to teach me how to do so.

I will treasure the exquisite simplicity, clarity & focus with which I lived. 

I will treasure how I made time for myself. 

I will treasure how I gave myself time to BE, rest, PLAY, explore, emerge & BLOSSOM. 

I will treasure the way I treasured my time, energy & attention.

in years to come, 

I will look back & say... 

I needed this. 
I needed that. 
I needed all of this.

I will treasure the way I chose myself.

I will treasure the way I backed myself. 

I will treasure the way I made time for myself.

“if no one was watching, would you live differently?” 

no. 

I know what it’s like to lose myself. 
I know what it’s like to find myself. 
I know what it’s like to create myself. 

this is the foundation & cornerstone of all else. 

this, right now: 

this moment.

I’m better alone than lonely. 

love is easy when I'm truthful. 

I've never been loved like this before. 

I've never felt so loved.
I've never felt so connected.

I've never felt so much sisterhood. 
I’ve never felt such a deep sense of belonging. 

it matters.
it’s everything.

14/2/2023

with you,the incongruence & lack of integrity = what stung.

you can think I am bad. 

you have a right to think what you like.

I won't try to stop you. 
I want you to be free too. 

I don't need to try to control you. 
it won't work, anyway.

I'm thinking about the tendency to hide or withhold information. 

the self-consciousness with which I used to operate. 

the judgements I am [still sometimes] faced with. 

it doesn't matter. 
none of it matters.

love is my greatest & purest expression. 

I love so deeply, with my whole heart, and I don't regret it. 

I have never been heartbroken in a way that didn't bring me closer to God. or to me--the real me-- by extension. 

I am so grateful for my ability to love.

I am not in love with a person. 

I'm in love with a feeling. 
I'm in love with myself. 

I'm in love with how alive I feel. 
I'm in love with how alive I get to be. 

I'm in love with what gets to be possible for me. 

I'm in love with me.

12/2/2023

tonight, 

I'm reflecting on my love for life itself, & what a blessing it is to be able to love like this. 

I see myself in everyone whose lives I touch. their eyes, their smiles, their laughs. I have nothing to fear.

I let a day flow. 

there is space, 
there is time,
there is grace. 

I welcome & include myself in that. 

the goal of this public journal was never to add to my to-do list.

it was “give myself space to emerge more fully”.

so, here I am ☀️

I am very, very lucky. 
my soul chose me too.

more letters...

P5: thank you for being there for me in the ways that you can be, and in ways that are--were-- wholly unexpected. 

our relationship feels weird, tender & beautiful. 

I’m grateful for the safe space you create for me, too.

now... 

I write as a means of catharsis, self-expression & connection at the same time. 

I write to bring us closer together. 
I share to bring us home to love. 

I write to unite my own disparate parts. I share to show I am [more than] “good enough”.

I used to say I wrote bc I wanted to make ppl feel something. 

I was obsessed with making ppl feel.angry about it, even.

how dare we all be so heartless? 
how dare we all be so disconnected? 

“I’m going to stab you in the heart with my words. you are never going to forget me.”

reflections: 

there’s something about writing in this way that feels so crisp & clean. 

you have no idea what (& who) I'm talking about. 

give me enough time & I might not either. 

but that doesn’t matter.

what matters is how it feels. 
what matters is how it touches us.

P4: 

The OG!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

BAAAAAAAAAAAAAAABE. 

pretty sure you don't look at my twitter, but you get a special mention. of course. 

I don't have favourites, but you're my fave. you're actually my fave. because I'm irrational like that. 😉 

I love seeing you find your way.

P3: 

I love you even when our connection feels disjointed (which is often). 

I love your commitment to repair. 
I love your willingness to GO THERE. 

I love how relentless you are (too). 

I love how powerful we are together.

P2: 

you blow my heart wide open with who you are & the depth of your love for me. the truth of it. I come to you with all my fears & longings & you hold me in them. being loved by you feels easy. effortless, even. 

I'm so fucking grateful to be able to call you a sister.

it feels fun, new, expansive. 

I think I'm changing. 

I like myself more now.

everything feels less weighted. 

it's easier to see you-as-you.

Person 1 (P1): 

I think you're gorgeous. 

as soon as I saw you, I was like... 

“I like him.” 

I wrote in my diary that I want to hang out with you again. & maybe even be friends with benefits. 

I'm new to wanting--or being available for-- that kind of thing.

2/2/2023 

today, 

I'm going back to the drawing board. 

I’m going back to writing pseudonymous letters. 

it feels simple, heartfelt & truthful. 

it feels like the best thing I can do for myself here, with you. 

so, without further ado...

I choose peace. 

I choose to let my heart rest. 

I choose to remind myself-- & remember-- who I really am. 

I choose to notice & honour the increasing legibility of my desires & calling. 

how would X [who already has X] show up? 

dissolving into the new dawn

I choose not to second-guess myself. 

I choose to take myself at face value, for all my whimsy & ridiculousness. 

I choose to treat life as a creative act, and my life as performance art. 

I choose love; I choose to love freely, fully, with my whole heart. 

I choose joy.

I choose to trust my gut. 
I choose to be discerning. 

I choose to notice who shows up when & how they do it. I choose to notice how people make me feel; how people’s presence (physically & otherwise) affect my energy. 

I choose attraction > chasing. 
I choose beauty > hiding.

I choose to forgive. 

not for them. for me.

I choose to feel deeply & completely & forgive with the same reverence. 

I choose to focus on what I want to see more of. I choose to focus my time, attention & energies on people who embrace all of me.

I am letting go of my resentments. 

I choose not to give my power away. I choose not to stay hurt. 

I choose power instead. 
I choose to be powerful instead. 
I choose to create win/win relationships in the process.

I am letting go of anything & everything I can’t reconcile myself. 

I am choosing love, grace & hope. 

I am letting go of my shame around being different. 

I love myself as I am. there is nothing unloveable about me.

I am letting go of the story that I am wounded or have wounds. 

I don’t want that story. I don’t need that story. I don’t like that story. 

that story doesn’t serve me anymore. 

so, it's gone.

I am letting go of the story that I am or would be unwanted as I am. 

I am loved as I am. deeply. 

—

I am letting go of the story that I feel too much. 

all my feelings are beautiful. being able to feel so deeply is a gift. it doesn’t make me weak; it makes me strong.

[now]

I don’t need to fight anymore. there is a rightness to speaking unarguably. my tender, deeply feeling self is as brave, strong & powerful as her badass, go-getting identical twin. 

what stories am I telling myself that I can let go of now?

today is the first day of my life. 
today is the best day of my life. 
I know that even better is to come.

words are a vessel. 

let's set sail towards new shores.

I see windows where there were once just walls. the light streams in & I fall to my knees as I bask in its radiance. it isn’t just that I see a way out. I see a way through. I see a way through for us all. & through the windows,so much natural beauty.trees. a valley.a waterfall.

why am I suddenly capable of this now? 

BECAUSE of everything that came before. BECAUSE of everything, tangible & intangible, that I worked so hard for. 

I will not shy away from how hard I've worked. 

this isn't hustle porn. 
it’s truthful.

what excites me is that now I can envisage a different world & way of being: 

one that flows effortlessly from this one... my labours, my struggle, my journey, my reclamation, borne fruit.

being a hermit is my safe mode. 

it's protective; I've needed it as a cocoon. 

I've needed space & time (very) physically alone to metabolize the profound changes that have occurred & GROW.

the buzz! the hubbub! 

I thrive here. 
I come alive here. 

with the right people...

THIS IS MY HOME! 

it feels so good to come out of my shell.

it's really good for me to be busy, active & out of the house. 

I had forgotten how much I love meeting people in person in a work context. 

it's so energizing for me. it's so much fun for me. it's made me remember how much I loved working with a team in an office.

I did lots of brave things today, and they were easy. 

by which I mean... 

I handled them with aplomb. 
I handled them with grace. 

I handled them with truth, courage & integrity. 

& I took care of myself along the way.

everything I do is a personal redemption arc.

of course, I wanted to trust the adults around me.

my core wound isn't just that no one protected me. 

it's that no one helped me. 

no one helped me when they could have done. 

because no one really saw me.

I stand by my choice to choose power. 

I know that nothing meaningful is ever accomplished alone… & no meaningful life is ever led alone. 

in the words of Ram Dass:

“we are all walking each other home.”

and, in the words of Taylor Swift… 

https://t.co/jRvLV7qiOS

I stand by this. 

I stand by my choice to see his humanity when it would’ve been so easy—& so fucking tempting— not to. 

I stand by my choice to see, love, laugh at & honour my own. 

I stand by my choice to choose love. I stand by my choice to choose connection.

when he asked me why, I said: “the focus of my life & work is relationship alchemy.these aren’t just words to me—it’s a calling. 
I believe that conflict is & can be a force for greater connection & intimacy.I wanted to give us the opportunity to see what we could make of this.”

there’s a story I haven’t told yet. I’ll tell it as a fragment. it’s about a man who didn’t treat me very well in a professional context. at the time, I gave him the benefit of the doubt. he seemed surprised.
I love how fully I let go & let live.

I love my willingness to try. 

I love my willingness to cock up first time. 

I love that I am no longer a perfectionist. 

I love that I am willing to laugh at myself. 

I love that I am willing to give myself— & others— the benefit of the doubt.

I no longer look outside myself for validation of my worthiness. 

and… 

I treasure the parts of me that do.

sometimes, it takes me a while to respond to messages too. it isn’t personal. I may be inept at times, but my ineptitude isn’t shameful. my ineptitude is adorable.

I'm not at Inbox Zero. 
I'm could be doing more. 
I could have done more. 
I could feel overwhelmed. 

instead, I'm... flowing. 

this is my life. 

I'm following what's alive. 
I'm going as fast as I can feel. 

I love myself first.

something has changed within me. or sneaked up on me: 

I don't feel like a Seeker anymore. 

I don't feel like I'm looking for anything anymore. 

it's already here. I'm already home. 

my only task? 

to settle (& soften) into Love... even more. 

it's my true nature.

I have never wanted to be ‘busy’ for the sake of it, but I used to fill time. 

I was lonely & isolated. 
I wanted to be like everyone else. 
I wanted to seem to be living a certain way. 

now, I see that (almost) everyone does that, and I can’t bear to be around it anymore.

my life is full. 

I'm not searching for anything outside myself anymore. 

I have all I want & need & more. 

if you really knew me, you’d know... 

I treasure being & feeling grounded. nothing is more important to me. there’s nowhere to be but here. life is spiritual practice.

this is real too. 

what makes me happy is... 

having the space & time & freedom to roam. relationships that feel effortless, or at least truly mutual & reciprocal. 

& perhaps there's a disconnect in my mind. erroneously, I associate Y with X & X with Y. or maybe it's just...

of course, it's personal. 

that's the point. 

it doesn't mean I don't like you or I doing care. 

it means, this isn't right. 

I'm not going to try & do something that isn't right. 

even if, at times, I still feel guilty about it.

as I end my day, what do I want to say?

what am I thinking? what am I feeling?what's alive? 

I'm reflecting on the power of no. 

the power of only saying yes to what I REALLY want. 

when I say no to something or someone, it usually means I don't want X enough. it's personal.

P4: 

thank you for making time for me. 

thank you for your commitment to me & this. thank you for not taking it & me for granted. 

thank you for your bravery. 
thank you for staying. 
thank you for seeing me, not objectifying me. 

thank you for loving me.

I love you too.

there is no conflict in paradox. 

it's where we reconcile ourselves with our humanity. it's how we see the world with new eyes. 

I am a juxtaposition of apparent/could-be opposites, and I wouldn't have it any other way. 

I'm grateful for it. I’m grateful for this time & space.

3. I’m not resisting being on my own side. I BACK MYSELF. 

I know what I want. 
I know what I value. 
I know what I deserve. 

I know you'd agree with me on all of this. and...

I HAVE FEELINGS TOO. I embody & contain multitudes. I can think/feel/be many things at the same time.

answers below: 

1. I'm resisting hearing... how much I liked you. how special you are/were to me. how sad it makes me feel that you aren't in my life 

2. I’m not resisting loving myself (I notice resistance to this question, lol. I love myself with a fervour that defies reason)

you make me feel vulnerable because you make me feel lonely. 

& I so rarely feel lonely nowadays. 

loneliness is such a fucking beautiful thing. 

back to my favourite coaching questions... https://t.co/sQC3VcZyEK

I need to say it. 

one day. in my own way. 

& sometimes, there's nothing else to say. 

you know it all already. you're ahead of me. you just play the game differently. 

we’re on the same team.

I had so much I wanted to say to you, and then I realised, “screw it, it doesn't matter.”

or rather: it can wait. 

it matters & it's not my focus. 
it matters & what matters most about it is what it represents. 

there's so much I didn't say. there's so much I couldn't say.

I love my anger. I love my rage. I love my frustration. 

I love being able to say what I mean & feel what I say. 

I love being able to share what I mean & mean what I share. 

I love being able to share from the heart.

he couldn't understand why I was so angry until I said that I missed you.

then, he got it. 

HE’S SO FUCKING SLOW! I said. 

I'm faster (& more relentless) than most, but still: w the speed comes pain. 

it isn't that I’m rushing my life. it's that I want you in it & I’m PISSED.

I fell in love with who I became with you. & then I became her without you. 

because, in practice, who I became w you = a pale imitation of who I am & will be. 

she was a starting point. we all have to start somewhere. 

humbly, I’m grateful for you. I'm grateful for it all.

I don't know how to talk about heartbreak. 

when I think about what I’m mourning with you, it isn't (really) you-as-you. it's everything that came with it. it's you-as-you, of course-- & it's also who I was with you.

P3: 

I cried on a call to another man about you today. I told him how heartbroken I was, and he felt it. to the point where it looked like he was kinda having a heart attack over it & I had to be like, “no, seriously, I'm okay.” 

words don't do Big Feelers justice.

it feels kind of cool & weird to be showing up differently. I feel like a big kid. a bit shy & nervous. I want to be liked & be approved of. I care about what you think of me. I am adorable & I treasure this part of me. I will keep her safe.

I don't need to recreate old dynamics with you. I don't need to drag you into other people’s dramas (or old stories/BS with other people). it doesn't matter. none of this matters. we’re here now. we’re never going back. & I for one have no desire to. I've learned too much.

P2: 

I feel nervous about seeing you again. 

I didn't send the message I drafted & I don't think I will. 

time passes, moods change, and... it just seems less important. 

or rather: I'm not afraid.

Person 1 (P1):

it's you! 

it's you, it's you, it's you. 

I don't even know who I'm writing this to. 

it just feels... instinctual. 

this is fun!

it's fun to press pause. 

I watch my mind whirring, write reams & reams of consciousness, and then enjoy keeping it to myself. 

here are some more letters I haven't sent (any relationship with pseudonyms used previously is incidental; see also: I'm lazy & I like mystery):

freedom is personal sovereignty, bodily autonomy & a felt sense of legitimacy. 

freedom is [in] the ability to roam. 

freedom is [in] the choice to forgive.

freedom is [in] the willingness to love. 

hatred is dissociative, which is why hatred thawed is heartbreak.

I value my freedom more than anything else in the world. 

mental. physical. spiritual. financial. 

what's mine is mine.
what's yours is yours. 

I didn't realise how fortunate I was to have been so profoundly not-free before. 

now, more than ever: I do. 

I really do.

everything ends, or at least changes... and I don't realise how lucky I am--or was-- until it happens. 

I am lucky. 
I feel lucky. 

I get to do what I want when I want with incredible people beside me. 

what more does anyone else (really) want?

things that came up for me today: 

giving myself the space & grace to change my mind without shame. 

I'm finding myself in new ways. 

I’m discovering myself in new ways. 

I've never felt so rooted. I like it. 
I've never felt this way before. 
I don't want it to end.

GRIEFGRIEFGRIEFGRIEFGRIEF

he said, “thanks for seeing me,” & in that moment, I felt seen by him too.perhaps for the first time.

this feels vulnerable to share. 

I told you I was into “performance art”, but not like this. 

this doesn't make sense. but rationality is overrated. 

I'm done with trying to fathom anything. 

I always knew more than I thought I did.
she loves me & she's proud of me.

can you grieve something you didn't love, or just something you didn't know you loved? 

I have grieved so much in my life. 

but I don't feel like I’ve lost anything. 

she needed to die, but she's still here. she's still here. she loves me & she's grateful for me.

nothing has changed & everything has changed. 

I didn't understand. 
I understand now. 

understanding hurts, but it isn't painful. I enjoy the pain not because I’m a masochist (I'm actually *really* not into pain, but that's another story), but because grief is love.

I felt the weight of how much I had changed. 

I felt my grief. 
& I feel it now, as I write this. 

grief is love. 
grief is love. 

she had to die so I could live. 

over & over & over again.
but this time, I didn't have to kill her. 

she died naturally.

to me, or anyone else.

expressing how I feel cleanly, clearly & directly is not “causing trouble”. 

it's being truthful. 

something that-- if I was really going to labour the point-- many people struggle to be & do.

I feel tender with you. there are so many what-ifs. one day, I’ll share our story-- or at least my side of it. thank you for inspiring me to be braver & bolder. I always wanted to be a romantic heroine. and so, I became her.

P8: 

my soul sister. (another soul sister) 

I miss the other one & I love you just as much. there is no comparison; you are different people. 

language is powerful. 
you ignite my protective instincts. 

when I'm around you, all I want to do is celebrate you & keep you safe.

P7: 

I feel your big, beautiful heart & it opens mine. I love how willing you are to challenge authority. I admire your dogged commitment to love & truth. it was deeply touching for me to see & feel your surprise when you realised I actually liked you-as-you.

P6:

when I thought about the kind of man I wanted to call into my life (in a romantic capacity), you were it. you were always it. you'll probably never read this. you probably already know this. it's cool. we’re all good at pretending not to know things. 😉

you're just there. you're solid; you're sturdy. I know you'll be there if & when I need you. most importantly, I know you'd actually want to be. I haven't needed you, and that doesn't escape me. your presence alone is wanted.

P5:

you are one of the tenderest, most sensitive & most loving men I've ever met. I’m so grateful for your caring presence in my life. I remember when I first met you, P4 & I were still sussing things out; I was in performance mode. in the months since, that's melted away.

P4: 

I want to shout from the rooftops about you. you're my fucking everything. I'm so grateful I found you. I'm so grateful we found one another. wow. no words. we are powerhouses alone & superstars together. I love you & I’m here to stay.

P3: 

I see & feel your humanity and it mingles with my own. it humbles me. you humble me. I see & feel your tenderness. I'm touched by your longing.

Person 2 (Person 2): 

you're fucking badass. you're so fucking badass. I can't tell you how much I love, respect & appreciate you. hats off to you. hats fucking off to you. you rule. I love it. I love you. thank you for everything. 

with you, I'm dissolving. 
do you see it yet?

Person 1 (P1): 

I kept your secret. 
I kept your secrets, plural. 

I hope you notice it. 
I hope you noticed it [then]. 

I didn't understand why I had to do it & I still did it. 

it isn't mine. it's yours.

if you recognise yourself, know that I love you. if you recognise someone else, know that I love them. 

savour it. tend to it.

listen to where the knowing wants you to go. 

let's soften.

language is powerful.

my eyes are tired. they sting.

these are the words of a warrior. 

these are the words of someone who has fought to be where & who she is. 

these are the words of a woman who loves without limits. 

these are the words of a woman who cares. 

I love you all. over & out 🫡

I see it now. 

I see THIS now. 

I see this in ways I couldn’t—didn’t— see before. 

I see this in ways I wasn’t ABLE to before. 

I see this in ways that fear hid me from. 

and I am so fucking proud of myself. 

I am so fucking proud of myself. 

over & over & over again.

I love the magic of Life. 

I love treating it as a puzzle. 

I’m obsessed with hacking myself. 
I’m obsessed with hacking this. 

and when I find it, and when I feel it… I feel infinite. one step at a time. one foot in front of the other. 

IT’S ALL HERE. it’s all here.

I LOVE MY DISCERNMENT. 

I love how easy it feels to share here. 

I love how open I feel. 
I love how safe I feel. 

I love that I feel no need to share specific details… and that it also doesn’t feel like I’m hiding or withholding anything. 

I love being able to hold it all.

I am amazed & I am humbled. 
I am humbled & I am grateful.

I am grateful & I am clear. 

I KNOW I have worked hard for this. I KNOW I am deserving. 

right place, right time— always. 

I’m ready in a new way.

I AM GOING TO HAVE SO MUCH FUN OVER THE NEXT FEW MONTHS! 

that’s what’s coming up for me now. 

I’m bubbling over with glee. trips, experiences, integration… I’m bubbling over with HOW GOOD IT GETS TO BE. how good it gets to feel. what gets to be possible for me.

that feels precious. 

I notice how good it feels to give myself this grace period. to be unflinching in what I want. to ask, and be met! to be clear. to EMBRACE MY RELENTLESSNESS. 

I fucking love my relentlessness. I am so proud of this. I am so proud of her. she is incredible.

I notice that night is a special time for me. everything feels so distinct, and alive, and beautiful, and CLEAR! everything feels so clear. I exult in it. I get my best ideas at night when I’m alone— just thinking, just being. at home. comforted. I am happy being myself here.

I am awesome. I love myself! I love myself so much! I am so loved! I am so celebrated! my heart is so warm. 

I have the best people in my life— people I can truly be myself with. and everything falls into place naturally as I let myself soften.

my biggest lessons of today are as follows: 

FOCUS ON WHAT’S RIGHT IN FRONT OF YOU

fuck me, I can make so much progress when that happens. when I do that. when I choose to do that. when I double down. 

I am SO FUCKING PROUD OF MYSELF! 

I am badass. I am resilient. I am bold.

if you expect to be taken seriously, you will behave like someone who is (expecting to be) taken seriously.
if you expect to be successful, you will behave like someone who is (expecting to be) successful.
if you expect to be worshipped, you will behave like someone who is (expecting to be) worshipped.
if you expect to be adored, you will behave like someone who is (expecting to be) adored.
if you expect to be celebrated you will behave like someone who is (expecting to be) celebrated.
if you expect to be disappointed, you will behave like someone who is (expecting to be) disappointed.
if you expect to be hurt, you will behave like someone who is (expecting to be) hurt.
if you expect to be rejected, you will behave like someone who is (expecting to be) rejected.
if you expect to be ignored, you will behave like someone who is (expecting to be) ignored.
if you expect to be attacked, you will behave like someone who is (expecting to be) attacked.

what would it take for you to be able to hear Her?

an existential question, and a philosophical one. 

I've discarded my earlier points.
or rather... they've blurred into one.

many truths can & do coexist in plain sight. 

in silence, we can hear. 

can you hear Her singing? 

there is no rush. 

there is nothing to search for or cling to. 

we are here. there is only here. 

can you hear Her now?

I have at least 10 women for every man. or so it feels like.

actually, I lie. 

what if this was right in front of me, in plain sight? 

what if I was (am) just resisting because I'm afraid & I don't know what & where to take the relationship/s & it all feels tentative, and...

I notice [where] I can still feel disappointed when other people don't follow through. 

I notice how much a part of myself wants to be able to look up to people, particularly men. 

to trust, respect & honour them. 

I have INCREDIBLE people like this in my life. 

and...

I notice that the number one thing that triggers me into a freeze state is feeling as if I need to perform (aka: I don't know what I'm doing). 

I notice myself get all shy & scared in those situations (even--especially-- when they ‘just’ occur within my own mind).

I notice that a part of me still (sometimes) expects to be treated like a child.

I notice that if & when I [emotionally] spiral here, I do so for the same reasons as always: I feel unseen, unheard & unloved (and I want to feel the opposite).

let's go through each one by one. 

and first... soften. 

I notice how strange it feels to share this stuff here. 

I notice how good I’ve become at purring my attention out. 

I notice how easy it feels to choose this; to choose my responses & state-shift at will.

here are some things that I’m thinking about right now: 

1. reciprocity & turn-taking in relationships

2. being told by a man who doesn't know me that I am hated by another woman who doesn't know me 

3. feminine archetypes

I’ve been thinking a lot about self-censorship. 

clearly, I'm writing for an audience now. that's inescapable. 

and... I don't want that to get in the way. 

I want to be with essence & stay with essence. 

I want to create a way for us all to stay.

[later] 

it's been a big day. 

I feel like I say that every day. and maybe I say it every day because it's true. it IS true. 

every day is a big day. every day is a new day. every day is beautiful. 

I'm on my way. and I’m already here. at exactly the same time.

closing out today with a deep sense of connection to something far beyond Me. I’m grateful to have the space to unfurl, dream, emerge & blossom in real-time with you. sweet dreams, friends 🫶

I'm going to sign off now & complete my evening ritual by writing a list of Good Things from the day. this isn't (consciously) a gratitude list, just a list of good things that have happened. sometimes I'll switch things up & add a few Things I Want To Remember as well.

the last few years taught me in so many different ways that I never am--or have to feel-- alone. I have myself & I have incredible people cheering me on. I'm building amazing things with people who make my heart sing. I'm happy; I've never known love like this before.

what fulfils me is partnering with incredible people to make my dreams-- our dreams-- come true. 

what fulfils me is being recognised & celebrated for my gifts. 

what fulfils me is being able to be my own best testimonial (& then some). 

I love being able to be [loved for] me.

how you say X is at least as important as what you say. 

how you do Y is at least as important as what you do. 

how you live Z is at least as important as what your life looks like or what you choose to do with it.

I relinquish any desire--or need--for control. 

I’ve been relinquishing all desire-- & need-- for control.

& the more I do this, the more effortless things are. the more effortless life is.

the more effortless my life gets to be.
you can take away my freedom but you can't take away my power. I am powerful & I will always be powerful. because I know how to be.
you can take away my power but you can't take away my freedom. I am free & I will always be free. because I know how to be.
afraid, yet I also knew that I hadn't always felt that way. I knew I had been afraid. I knew I WAS born with the fear gene. and I knew that something had changed. I felt it, I feel it, irrevocably.

a few days ago, I was speaking with a close friend (& biz partner-- SO MUCH EPIC STUFF TO COME AROUND THIS, OMG) about this. she said something about us “not having the fear gene”, and it stuck with me. in that moment, I realised I couldn't remember what it was like to feel truly

I’ve been tiptoeing around the edges of writing like this for some time because I’ve been worried about being (or seeming) “too much”. I already tweet a lot, and don't need to *try* to stand out in any way, shape or form: I already do. it can be scary to break the mould.

speaking to you clarifies my thought process. having you as an audience allows me to see myself with new eyes. how could I ever be or feel alone? how could I ever have felt alone? it's all so silly (& so beautiful). & I say that with love.

I want to use this space to talk. like, really talk. & be (even) more integrated. I want to use this space to speak to you as I would-- as I do-- myself. I want you to know me & I want you to feel me. because that's where the magic is; that's when the magic happens.

no one becomes prolific by accident. 

I am safe & warm & tucked up in bed ~midnight on a Friday night, and I feel like the luckiest girl (woman) in the world. 

loving myself grounds me like nothing & no one else. this is priceless. this is what I always wanted.

I want to do so in a way that allows me to see & be seen... & know & be known. differently. in a new way. 

I'm free-writing here. 

when I said this was going to be a diary entry, I wasn't joking. 

I LOVE writing. & every time I hold myself back from sharing here, it hurts me.

13/1/2023

I want to stretch how open I can allow myself to be here. 

I want to stretch how open I feel safe to be here. 

& I want to do so in a way that honours me.

I've wanted to be able to be ME. and I am me, and I love me, and as the tears roll down my cheeks I am inspired & rejuvenated. I am reminded of what it means to be alive. I am reminded of what it means to be free. I am proud of myself. I have nothing to hide. I feel clean.

I can feel it. I can feel you. these words are word vomit in the sink. but they’re important, and I relish this. I’ve waited so long to speak. I've never wanted to hurt anyone. I’ve just wanted to be able to BE.

and so, I am not granted humanity. I am not granted the space to breathe & feel. I am not allowed to take up space. I am not allowed to rage. you control me without meaning to simply because [I know] you are afraid.

learning that the adults around me didn't know any better than me was the most painful experience of my life. it blasted me open. I can't remember when I learned this,but it’s been a bittersweet driving force of the last 20 years. I see through you. you're terrified of me.

I was seven when I [was] shut down. I was seven when I shut myself down. I was so smarter-- smarter than I am now, in many ways. I had no idea how smart I was. it was instinctual. I did what I needed to do to survive. I shut myself down; I stayed small; I stayed quiet.

I want to talk about how it feels to have the people around you, who are supposed to protect you, turn a blind eye to what's going on. I want to talk about what it’s like to be a child when this happens. old beyond your years, yet young.

I have already broken all the rules. I broke them because I had to. I broke them because it was the only way I could free myself. I don't want to talk about what happened. I want to talk about why it matters.

I am the daughter of parents who did the best they could. I grew up in a world where children should be seen & not heard. my story is intrinsically intermingled in & with other people’s. what can I share without ruffling (more) feathers? does it really matter anymore?

I want you to be able to understand what it takes & has taken to be able to say [& do] any of this. by writing this, I have betrayed everyone I love. by speaking out, I “make other people feel uncomfortable”... & I do so knowingly. to some, I could be The Murderer of a Dream.
I want to be able to speak my truth without shame or apology.

I want you to know why it's taken me so long to speak out & why I’ve spoken out in such convoluted ways at times. I want you to be able to attempt to understand where I am coming from. I want you to know that I am coming from a place of love, not pain.
I hate these words. they make me cry. I want you to know that as I share; I want you to be able to feel this & feel me. I want you to know that I don't say any of this lightly.

the best thing about this experience is that I know he didn’t mean it. by which I mean, [I know] he didn’t mean to hurt me. I know he didn’t know any better. his actions showed me conclusively that I didn’t want to go any further w him. it’s over. I’m safe. “lucky” even (again).

there’s a thin line between love & hate but ambivalence is icy. my equanimity is warm, loving & stone-cold all at once. when you are dead to me, you are dead to me. it’s personal & not-personal at the same time. it’s also irreversible.

he asked me what he could do to make it up to me. 

I said I’d let him know. 

I said he owes nothing to me & owed nothing to me. I meant it. 

I had already forgiven him. bc he was already nothing to me. & perhaps that was the most tragic thing in his eyes. 

I knew he liked me.

and in that moment, I forgave him.

I forgave him because I didn’t just shrink back & stay silent. 

and I didn’t just get up & leave with no explanation. 

I was clear, emphatic & grounded. 

I owned my shit & I spoke with conviction. 

I forgave him bc I took care of myself.

if you can’t lie next to me without trying to touch me intimately you are never, ever, ever lying next to me again. 

I got up & left.
because it is. it was. clear as day. no ifs, no buts, no plausible deniability.

by that point… 

I may have been half-asleep & exhausted, but I was also fucking lucid. 

I said, “what do you think you’re doing?” 

and then, “you do realise that what you just did is sexual assault, right?”

my body is mine, not yours. 

thought process: “OF COURSE I’m fucking angry with you, idiot. you violated me. and now you don’t want me to be angry with you? SERIOUSLY? because it’s your feelings that matter [more] here?”

I’m going to talk about the things that are (or should be) really, really, really obvious. 

like… 

don’t touch someone intimately while they’re sleeping unless you’ve discussed it beforehand & you’re 110% sure that they [would] want it.
and I’m so fucking proud of myself for that. and I will shout from the rooftops about that. and nothing & no one will stop me.

it wasn’t. it unequivocally wasn’t. I’m talking about this to make a v v v important point. I’m talking about this now because the last time anything like this happened, I was silent about it for months. I’m talking about it now because I do things differently now [too].

I hate these words [“SA”]. 

that’s the first bit. 

my skin crawls as I say them. 

there’s a rage that comes with the unfurling. 

I’m [still] bracing to be attacked. 

I’m [still] bracing to be told it was my fault—by whom, I don’t know.
